steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the text makes about the people at the gathering. The key evidence is: each person's strokes "had gone awry" "for some reason which he could not grasp," and afterward each turned to a neighbor to privately rectify what was said publicly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Each enjoys speaking publicly about abstract issues but dislikes speaking privately about them.': reverses the text. Each person spoke publicly then turned to a neighbor to clarify — the private rectification is presented as a natural follow-up, not something they dislike. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Each fails at presenting a wholly coherent vision of art but does not understand why.': matches both key details — strokes "had gone awry" (failure to present coherently) and "for some reason which he could not grasp" (does not understand why). ✓
  • Choice C — 'Each is contemptuous of the other attendees but strives to impress them.': no contempt appears anywhere in the passage; the text only describes confusion and private correction. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Each becomes absorbed in a question about art, and no one knows how to answer the question.': the passage shows each person proposing a conception of art, not grappling with a shared unanswered question. The confusion is about delivery, not the question itself. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
